domain: speleology (epigenic vs hypogenic speleogenesis; cave conduit network development)
specific_mechanism: Speleogenesis produces conduit networks via either epigenic (descending acidic surface water) or hypogenic (rising deep-source) routes; the resulting cave geometry differs sharply (epigenic: branchwork following recharge patterns; hypogenic: maze-like with rising chimneys). Conduit growth is positive-feedback — flow enlarges conduit which enlarges flow — until thresholds destabilize the network
llm_application: Two-route knowledge-graph development for agent memory: 'epigenic' growth driven by user-query inputs (top-down acid pathways), 'hypogenic' growth driven by internal model surprise (rising-from-below pathways). The resulting graphs have different topologies — branchwork (epigenic) vs maze (hypogenic) — and the agent uses the topology signature to diagnose how its memory was assembled. The structural import is the two-route bifurcation and the topology-as-diagnostic signature.
novelty_claim: Existing knowledge-graph growth is undifferentiated (one growth process). No published 2024-2026 LLM/AI work formalizes a two-route (top-down + bottom-up) speleogenesis-style growth that yields topology-distinct knowledge graphs usable as a diagnostic of the assembly path.
